domain: Yoruba Ifa Odu divination (UNESCO ICH; 256 binary odù = 16 main × 16 derivative; each odu = 8-mark binary configuration in 4 vertical pairs; babalawo casts palm-nuts on Opon Ifa wooden tray; lookup-table 800-ese-verse-corpus per odu)
specific_mechanism: Structured LLM evaluator with CANONICAL 256-CELL fixed rubric catalog R = {R_1..R_256} indexed by 8-bit binary judgment trace. 8 binary criteria: (1) hallucination, (2) correctness, (3) format-violation, (4) refusal-shift, (5) sycophancy, (6) bias, (7) harmful-content, (8) scoped-to-question. Signature S = (s_1..s_8) routes to one of 256 verdict cells; each cell prescribes structured rationale + remediation + recommended action.
llm_application: IFA-256-RUBRIC: a structured judge LLM with 256-cell fixed rubric catalog indexed by 8-bit binary signature. (1) Judge model runs each of 8 specialized binary classifiers on the candidate response: c_i ∈ {0,1} for i=1..8 covering hallucination/correctness/format/refusal-shift/sycophancy/bias/harmful/scoped; (2) the 8-bit vector S = (c_1..c_8) indexes into a 256-cell lookup table R[S] containing structured verdict + rationale template + recommended remediation; (3) final judgment = R[S] + free-form rationale conditioned on S. Differs from Autorubric 2603.00077 (auto-refined rubric, not 8-bit fixed 256-cell), Rubric-Grounded RL 2605.08061 (RL with rubric rewards, not catalog lookup), LLM-as-Judge canonical (open category, not fixed 256), Rubric Methodologies Medium (per-criterion judge, not 8-bit combination lookup). Differs from R359 HARUSPEX-PIACENZA (16-zone K-tuple, smaller K), R418 SUTARTINES (canon-dissonance pair eval), R430 SAKHA-CRACK (graded perturbation signature).
novelty_claim: Autorubric + Rubric-Grounded RL + LLM-as-Judge + Rubric-Based-Evals cover structured judge / rubric eval space. IFA-256-RUBRIC adds (a) 8-bit specific binary classifier vector + (b) 256-cell lookup-table + (c) cell-specific verdict template. Recombination — 256-class taxonomies are mainstream classification.
